scope: Desktop UI Item AI Interaction Flow
interaction_flow: User → UIItem(ContextMenu): right-click file(s) → clicks 'Open in AI' menu item
parseItemMetadataForAI → buildAIPayloadFromItems: returns parsed JSON object | undefined (on parse failure)
buildAIPayloadFromItems → sendSelectionToAIApp: returns items[] [{uid, path, name, is_dir, is_shortcut, shortcut_to, shortcut_to_path, size, type, modified, metadata}]
ensureAIAppIframe → launch_app: launch_app({ name: 'ai' }) [only if AI window not found in DOM]
launch_app → ensureAIAppIframe: resolves (or throws on failure)
ensureAIAppIframe: if window still not found → return null
ensureAIAppIframe → sendSelectionToAIApp: returns iframe (.window-app-iframe element) | null
sendSelectionToAIApp: if aiIframe === null || !aiIframe.contentWindow → call UIAlert({ message: i18n('ai_app_unavailable') })
sendSelectionToAIApp → AIAppIframe(contentWindow): postMessage({ msg: 'ai:openFsEntries', items, source: 'desktop-context-menu' }, '*')
key_relationships: buildAIPayloadFromItems calls parseItemMetadataForAI (for each DOM element)
ensureAIAppIframe calls launch_app (conditional: only if AI window absent from DOM)
sendSelectionToAIApp calls UIAlert (error path: when iframe unavailable)
UIItem (ContextMenu) triggers sendSelectionToAIApp from two call sites: multi-select and single-item right-click
AppService (backend) manages app registry/lifecycle; not directly invoked in this UI flow

method_signatures.buildAIPayloadFromItems: buildAIPayloadFromItems($elements: jQuery): Array<{uid, path, name, is_dir, is_shortcut, shortcut_to, shortcut_to_path, size, type, modified, metadata}>

conditions_and_branching: AI window not in DOM → launch_app('ai') called; if still absent → return null
aiIframe === null || no contentWindow → UIAlert('ai_app_unavailable') shown, flow terminates
metadata not parseable as JSON → console.warn + returns undefined (non-fatal)
Two context menu trigger sites: (1) multi-select via $selected_items, (2) single-item via $(el_item)
important_notes: All three AI functions (parseItemMetadataForAI, buildAIPayloadFromItems, sendSelectionToAIApp) are module-level constants in UIItem.js, not class methods. AI_APP_NAME = 'ai'. Communication to the AI app is purely via cross-frame postMessage with no return value. AppService.js (backend) is unrelated to this client-side flow — it manages server-side app CRUD and is not called here.
